text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Laurel Meadows, there lived a girl named Olive who had an enormous heart for animals. Every morning before her classes at the local university, Olive would wander through the fields chasing butterflies, pet birds, and catching frogs for a cozy evening pond hop.

Her best friend was a wise old owl named Oliver. Sometimes, they'd fly off together into the sunset, mapping out bird migration routes over the hills. Olive found joy in everything Oliver did.

One peculiar afternoon, as Olive was arranging some flowers, she heard a faint tweet-tweeting nearby. Her hawk gaze turned toward a big branch.

There was a turtle there! Oliver swooped down and landed beside him with a soft 'tick'. They chatted away about things they loved—owlhoots, obviously. Olive fed the turtle chocolate ants until it happily flew away.

That evening, while eating sundresses she made, Olive imagined how Oliver could fly. She wouldn't dare tell him that—it wouldn
